title: calystegia purpurata subsp. saxicola
intro: Calystegia purpurata subsp. saxicola, also known as a type of morning glory vine, is a plant species native to the coastal regions of New South Wales and Queensland in Australia. The medicinal properties of this plant have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Calystegia purpurata subsp. saxicola include its antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory issues.
